KeyValue.value: Layout Growth Test
Verify the layout growth looks correct when adding buttons.\n1. Add 4 buttons to the newly created frame.\n2. Verify that they layout as expected.
Wise:CreateGroup('Debug Growth Bar', 'line'); Wise.frames['Debug Growth Bar']:Show(); Wise:ToggleEditMode()
Wise:DeleteGroup('Debug Growth Bar'); Wise:ToggleEditMode()
Options Panel Initialization

Verify that the settings panel opens correctly.
1. Open Options
2. Navigate to 'Settings'
3. Check that 'Icon Size' is displayed.
if not Wise.OptionsFrame then Wise:CreateOptionsFrame() end; Wise.OptionsFrame:Show(); if Wise.SelectTab then Wise:SelectTab('Settings') end
if Wise.OptionsFrame then Wise.OptionsFrame:Hide() end
Cooldowns Decimal Slots Test

Verify that adding a decimal slot to the Cooldowns interface persists across logins.
1. Enable the Cooldowns interface.
2. Open actions for Cooldowns and add a slot like 1.5 with any ability.
3. Reload the UI (/reload).
4. Verify that the decimal slot and ability are still present in the Cooldowns interface.

if not WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'] then return end; WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'].visibilitySettings = WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'].visibilitySettings or {}; WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'].visibilitySettings.customShow = '[always]'; Wise:UpdateGroupDisplay('Cooldowns')
if not WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'] then return end; WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'].visibilitySettings.customShow = ''; Wise:UpdateGroupDisplay('Cooldowns')
Hide Native Interface Test

Verify that checking the 'Hide Game Interface' box in the Properties panel hides the native interface.
1. Enable the Cooldowns interface in settings.
2. Open Properties for Cooldowns.
3. Check the 'Hide Game Interface' box and verify the default UI element (EssentialCooldownViewer) hides.
if not WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'] then return end; WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'].hideNativeInterface = true; Wise:UpdateCooldownWiser('Cooldowns', 'EssentialCooldownViewer')
if not WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'] then return end; WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'].hideNativeInterface = false; Wise:UpdateCooldownWiser('Cooldowns', 'EssentialCooldownViewer')
Auto-Loaded Utilities Persistence Test

Verify that auto-loaded spells in Utilities persist when the native interface is hidden.
1. Enable the Utilities interface.
2. Ensure auto-loaded spells are present.
3. Hide the game interface in Utilities properties.
4. Reload UI.
5. Verify spells are still there.
if not WiseDB.groups['Utilities'] then return end; WiseDB.groups['Utilities'].hideNativeInterface = true; Wise:UpdateCooldownWiser('Utilities', 'UtilityCooldownViewer')
if not WiseDB.groups['Utilities'] then return end; WiseDB.groups['Utilities'].hideNativeInterface = false; Wise:UpdateCooldownWiser('Utilities', 'UtilityCooldownViewer')
Auto-Loaded Cooldowns Persistence Test

Verify that auto-loaded spells in Cooldowns persist when the native interface is hidden.
1. Enable the Cooldowns interface.
2. Ensure auto-loaded spells are present.
3. Hide the game interface in Cooldowns properties.
4. Reload UI.
5. Verify spells are still there.
if not WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'] then return end; WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'].hideNativeInterface = true; Wise:UpdateCooldownWiser('Cooldowns', 'EssentialCooldownViewer')
if not WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'] then return end; WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'].hideNativeInterface = false; Wise:UpdateCooldownWiser('Cooldowns', 'EssentialCooldownViewer')
Wiser Interfaces in Action Picker Test

Verify that 'Specs', 'Professions', 'Forms', and 'Menu Bar' appear in the Interface tab of the Action Picker.
1. Open Options.
2. Select or create an Interface.
3. Add an action and open the Action Picker.
4. Search for 'Interface' category.
5. Verify that 'Specs', 'Professions', 'Forms', and 'Menu Bar' are available as selectable options.
Cooldowns: Dynamic Spec Persistence
Switch specs. Verify that Cooldowns from your previous spec do not incorrectly appear in the new spec. Verify the actions are correctly tagged with the new specRequirements.

local group = WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns']; if not group or not group.actions then print('Cooldowns group not initialized yet.') return end; local foundSpecSpells = 0; local foundGlobalSpells = 0; for _, states in pairs(group.actions) do for _, state in ipairs(states) do if state.autoLoaded then if state.category == 'spec' and type(state.specRequirements) == 'table' then foundSpecSpells = foundSpecSpells + 1 elseif state.category == 'global' then foundGlobalSpells = foundGlobalSpells + 1 end end end end; print(string.format('Found %d Spec-Restricted Spells, %d Global Spells.', foundSpecSpells, foundGlobalSpells)); if foundSpecSpells ~= 0 then print('Test Passed: Dynamically loaded spells are properly spec-restricted.') else print('Note: If you are above level 10 and have a spec, you should see spec-restricted spells.') end
Spec and Equipment Changer Test

Verify that the Spec and Equipment Changer Wiser Interface appears in the Options UI.
1. Open Options.
2. In the Sidebar, look under 'Wiser Interfaces'.
3. Click 'Spec and Equipment Changer'.
4. Verify slots for specs, talent loadouts, and equipment sets appear.
if not Wise.OptionsFrame then Wise:CreateOptionsFrame() end; Wise.OptionsFrame:Show(); Wise.selectedGroup = 'Spec and Equipment Changer'; Wise:UpdateOptionsUI()
if Wise.OptionsFrame then Wise.OptionsFrame:Hide() end
Show/Hide Rules UI Update Test

Verify the layout and text of the Availability Filtering button in Properties.
1. Open Options and select any interface.
2. Select an action in the middle panel.
3. Look at the Properties panel on the right.
4. Verify 'Availability Filtering' is the title and the text above the button no longer overlaps the 'Edit Filters...' button.
5. Click 'Edit Filters...' and verify the labels 'Show' and 'Hide' are used instead of 'Enable'/'Disable'.
Special Action bars: Leave Vehicle Action

Verify that 'Leave Vehicle', 'Extra Action Button 1', 'Zone Ability', 'Override Bar', and 'Possess Bar' are shown in the 'Special Action bars' category.
1. Open Options.
2. Select or create an Interface.
3. Add an action and open the Action Picker.
4. Change Category to 'Special Action bars'.
5. Verify that the actions are in the list.
Bar Copy: Viewer Performance Test

Verify the optimized Bar Copy tool for viewer-based bars (like Cooldowns).
1. Ensure the Cooldowns interface is active and has auto-loaded spells.
2. Open Options -> Sidebar -> Tools -> Bar Copy Tool.
3. Select 'Cooldown Manager Bar' as the source.
4. Select a new target interface.
5. Click 'Copy & Append'.
6. Verify that the spells are correctly copied and appended to the target interface.
7. Check the Lua console for any errors.
if not Wise.OptionsFrame then Wise:CreateOptionsFrame() end; Wise.OptionsFrame:Show(); Wise.selectedGroup = 'Bar Copy Tool'; Wise:UpdateOptionsUI()
if Wise.OptionsFrame then Wise.OptionsFrame:Hide() end
Addons Configuration Test

Verify that DataBroker plugins and Addon options appear in the Action Picker.
1. Open Options and create a new interface.
2. Add a new action and open the Action Picker.
3. Search or filter for 'Addons' category.
4. Verify that installed DataBroker plugins and Addon slash commands appear in the list.
5. Select one, place it on the bar, and click it out of combat to verify it opens the addon options or triggers the LDB action.
Addons Wiser Interface Test

Verify that the dynamic 'Addons' Wiser Interface is created and populated.
1. Open Options.
2. In the Sidebar, look under 'Wiser Interfaces'.
3. Click 'Addons'.
4. Verify the slots contain installed Addon slash commands and DataBroker plugins.
if not Wise.OptionsFrame then Wise:CreateOptionsFrame() end; Wise.OptionsFrame:Show(); Wise.selectedGroup = 'Addons'; Wise:UpdateOptionsUI()
if Wise.OptionsFrame then Wise.OptionsFrame:Hide() end
Empty Slots Highlight
1. Hover over the middle slot (the empty slot). It should NOT highlight or scale.\n2. Verify the empty slot acts as a spacer.
if Wise.frames['Test_EmptySlots'] then Wise.frames['Test_EmptySlots']:Hide() end; WiseDB.groups['Test_EmptySlots'] = nil
